blocks.text: Ciao a tutti.

todays my birthday wow. I'm 23 years old. It feels so old to me. 🥹. this last week has been a bike tour break, I've been driving around Genova with a motorcycle. I traded the bicicleta out for a real bike. I'll eventually get to sharing some stuff with you about it.
on to the 7th..
July 7th
Awwww the beauty of hostels. I slept in yet again, and in a bed too. I love it!
I left my bed to go to the common area to write and catch up on these posts.

That's the thing with hostels that I love so much... it's so easy to talk to people and meet new people. Within the first 5 minutes of writing I had talked with a group of Belgians and one French guy and we were all going to breakfast together.

This breakfast turned into a full day walking around Napoli. We got some pizza for lunch, I helped them moved their suitcases from the Hostel to an Airbnb since they had to check out of the hostel. After this I suggested we go to the subterranean underground of Napoli which was recommended to me by an Italian guy in a small village. So we went on a two hour tour of the underground of Napoli together.
Underneath Napoli was sooo cool. Literally the temperature was 18 C which was perfect since outside was 35 C.

Also we had an amazing tour guide. Usually I hate guided tours but the tour guide was super excited about the tour, she was very easy to listen to and you could tell that she loved giving these tours.
I learned so much about the underground of Napoli.

items: It was first used to bring water from nearby sources to the city so people could drink water
Then the water got polluted by a sewage system built on top and the city shut down the water system
After this it was used a garbage dump for the city. The city put thousands and thousands of tons of garbage down. Metres high piles of garbage in the whole cave system
Then World War Two Italy needed bomb shelters, since Napoli was the most bombed city in Italy in the war. SO they dumped concrete over the garbage so everyone could stand down there.
During the war up to 5000 to 10000 people crammed into the subterranean area to avoid bomb attacks

blocks.text: After the tour we started to chat with the tour guide. She was our age and we told her how amazing she was at giving tours.
We ended up inviting her to come with us for drinks. We all went and chatting it was fun.
Other memories of the day are

items: Waking around Napoli and feeling the pleasant energy of the city
Eating pizza and getting overcharged 10 euros.... I asked the waitress to pick me a pizza off the menu, and she picks the most expensive one then adds 5 extra things without telling me...
Drinking lots of coffee all the time. I love espresso.

blocks.text: Fast forward to the evening.
I got back to the hostel and got ready to go out. It was me my Argentino friend, The French guy and the Belgian girls, One more French girl joined us as well.
We all went out for drinks at some bar.

It was so packed!! Everyone was in the street. One of my favourite nights out. I loved the energy of the people on the street. I think I had too much to drink. My Argentinian friend and I would take turns buying each other round after round of drinks.

I was switching often between Italian, French, Spanish, and English. I had to act as a translator many times. I loved how international it was and also how I got to speak in all these language. This is extremely fun for me. It's like a game switching between them and you never get bored.
I got back at around 5 am and went to bed.
This was my last night in Napoli tomorrow I was going to leave and continue north to Roma.
More memories,

items: I spent way to much money compared to while I was biking and camping. I need to spent less.
When we went out for drinks I only drank water. It was 5 euros for a simple beer and I was not going to pay that.

The cab ride home was a disaster and we ended up not being able to pay. He had a card machine but would not accept our cards and demanded cash. He even wanted to drive us to many ATMs but they were closed.
I used Italian to haggle down cab prices. They don't make it easy lol.

blocks.text: That's all for now,
Erik

blocks.caption: Chapel that you see on every corner of Naples
Pizza for lunch. (My surprise 20 euro pizza🙃🙃)
Everyone on the street at night.

blocks.text: Ciao for now,
